title: Best-of-breed CLI --help + UX for an agent-invoked reflecting CLI — landscape + reusable elements
question: What do best-of-breed CLIs do for --help and overall CLI UX, and which concrete, reusable elements should a globally-installed, agent-invoked reflecting CLI (pi-context <op>, ~48 ops auto-derived from a typed op-registry) adopt to up-level help across ALL commands — primary consumer an LLM agent reading terminal output, secondary a human.
method: grounded online research (WebSearch + WebFetch of canonical guidelines, exemplar CLIs, and help-generation frameworks; every external claim cited with URL + retrieval date; two fetch failures disclosed not fabricated) plus pattern synthesis for the reflection-over-op-registry case.
scope: exemplars: gh / kubectl / docker / cargo / git / stripe-heroku-salesforce(oclif)
findings_summary: Every loved CLI's help converges on the SAME reusable 3-part architecture, which maps directly onto a reflection-over-op-registry CLI: (1) a render TEMPLATE with fixed slots (synopsis -> summary -> flags -> examples -> related -> footer; clap help_template, cobra SetUsageTemplate, bettercli); (2) a GROUP registry mapping each command to a named, ordered group (cobra AddGroup/GroupID — the gh CORE COMMANDS engine; note clap canNOT group subcommands, so op-grouping is custom render work, not a free framework feature); (3) a per-op EXAMPLES field surfaced verbatim (oclif examples, clap after_help, click epilog). Highest-leverage reusable elements to build ONCE and apply across all ~48 ops + every <op> --help: a grouped top-level listing using the SHORT one-liner (our currently-unused promptSnippet) instead of the multi-sentence description dump (gh/kubectl/docker/cobra; fixes today's flat unscannable help); a fixed per-op template with required-vs-optional TYPED flags and a docopt-notation SYNOPSIS line (an LLM already reads docopt); copy-pasteable common-first EXAMPLES (clig.dev 'lead with examples'; the single highest-value element for an agent caller); a RELATED/see-also hint (the natural surface for the currently-undiscoverable pi-bound); --version (+ -V); help-on-no-args and -h-after-anything -> STDOUT; and machine-readable help rendered through the same --format knob. The agent-primary framing is load-bearing, not secondary: practitioner sources state 'agents read --help before docs' and 'agents learn patterns faster from examples than flag descriptions', and require required-vs-optional shown explicitly ('agents will not guess'), structured/parseable help, stdout-for-data/stderr-for-messages, meaningful exit codes (0/1/2/3/4/5) paired with a parseable error_type + recovery hint, and token-economy output (--raw/--compact). clig.dev + 12-factor supply the baseline rules (-h everywhere idempotently; concise help when args missing; format for scannability with TTY-degradation honoring NO_COLOR/TERM=dumb/--no-color; --json/--plain; help to stdout). ALL SIX points once framed as open design forks are DERIVABLE from the CLI/pi-context facts, NOT user-calls: (a) group labels + ordering derive from the op-name partition — Read/Query (read-*/resolve-*/find-references/filter-*/join-*/walk-*), Block-writes (append/update/upsert/remove-block-item + nested, write-block), Relations (append/remove/replace-relation(s)), Schema&config (read/write-schema*/amend-config/read-config/rename-canonical-id), Substrate-lifecycle (context-*/update), Workflow (complete-task/resolve-conflict/promote-item), Process-mode (pi-bound) — ordered read-before-mutate -> vocabulary -> lifecycle -> process-mode last, implemented as a PURE CLASSIFIER over the op name (a new op auto-slots; never a hardcoded curated list, which is the drift-prone parallel list gap-arc-coherence + the parity gate exist to prevent); (b) color defaults PLAIN (agent-primary caller; honor NO_COLOR/TERM=dumb/--no-color); (c) machine help renders through the EXISTING --format (text|json|table, already shipped) — no parallel --help --json; (d) the one-liner reuses the existing promptSnippet (audit the ~48, fix any that read poorly; no new field); (e) all ~48 examples are hand-authored now (best-of-breed/no-deferral mandate; a synthetic floor is the rejected ship-after tier); (f) pi-bound is its own discoverable group entry with authored help (process-mode, not a reflected op). The circulating ~10-32x CLI-vs-MCP token/reliability multiplier is unverified landscape signal, not load-bearing.

external_refs: https://docopt.readthedocs.io/en/0.4.0/ (2026-06-09; docopt.org direct fetch FAILED, TLS cert)
https://cli.github.com/manual/ (2026-06-09; group strings via secondary sources)

stale_conditions: the cited web sources change or a fetched guideline/exemplar revises its recommendations

the derived design (group-by-name-prefix classifier; plain color default; help through --format; promptSnippet one-liner; per-op examples; pi-bound as an authored discoverable entry) is superseded by a different decided design
clap/cobra/oclif help-generation APIs change such that the mirrored template/group/examples mechanisms differ
pi-context's op-registry gains a native help-grouping or summary/examples field that supersedes the render-layer approach

citations.label: full research with per-claim guideline/exemplar/synthesis tags + source list
writing CLI tools that AI agents actually want to use
